import org.slf4j.Logger;

// JV006 (double capture, port of ERC005): no execution path may both report the
// caught (a tier-1 / tier-2 capture) and rethrow it. The upstream handler reports
// the propagated exception a second time, inflating the error backend. Each path
// picks one: report and swallow, or propagate without reporting. A stringified
// rethrow still reaches upstream carrying the failure, so it is still a double.
// Guard/branch shapes live in Jv006GuardShapes; every case here is linear.
class Jv006DoubleCapture {
    private final Logger log;

    Jv006DoubleCapture(Logger log) {
        this.log = log;
    }

    void reportThenRethrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006"
            log.error("failed", e);
            throw e;
        }
    }

    void reportThenWrap() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006"
            log.error("failed", e);
            throw new RuntimeException(e);
        }
    }

    // Adversarial: the rethrow stringifies the caught, but a fresh exception still
    // reaches upstream (JV006) and the object chain is broken (JV002) - both fire.
    void reportThenStringifiedRethrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) { // want "JV006"
            log.error("failed", e);
            throw new RuntimeException(e.getMessage()); // want "JV002"
        }
    }

    // Report only, no rethrow: a single capture, clean.
    void reportOnly() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("failed", e);
        }
    }

    // Propagate only, no capture: clean.
    void wrapOnly() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            throw new RuntimeException(e);
        }
    }

    // The rethrow is a fresh exception that does not carry the caught: not a
    // double (JV006 needs the caught to propagate), though it drops the cause
    // (JV002). JV006 must stay silent here.
    void reportThenUnrelatedThrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error("failed", e);
            throw new IllegalStateException("unrelated"); // want "JV002"
        }
    }

    // A print is not a tier-1 / tier-2 capture, so print + rethrow is not a
    // double - the error backend never sees it twice.
    void printThenRethrow() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
            throw e;
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
